programme.desc: Good Afternoon Britain

programme.title: Pretty radical: a young woman's journey into the heart of Poland's far right
programme.desc: We follow 19-year-old Paulina, who joins Poland's far-right National Radical Camp. She is quickly selected as a candidate in local elections because she's ‘a pretty young woman' who might improve the image of an organisation thought to be full of ‘bald hooligans vandalising the city'. What drew her to join the nationalist group in the first place? Will anything make her question her loyalty to them?

programme.title: Owen Jones meets ...: Owen Jones Meets
programme.desc: Owen Jones is a Guardian columnist and the author of Chavs: The Demonisation of the Working Class and The Establishment – And How They Get Away With It.

programme.title: Desert Fire
programme.desc: Football's world cup for unrecognised territories is viewed through the eyes of the manager and players of one of the most fascinating teams, Iraqi Kurdistan.

programme.title: Right to fight: Martial arts fighter with Down's syndrome
programme.desc: Garrett Holeve (aka G Money) has always dreamt of being a Mixed Martial Arts (MMA) fighter, and with the full support of his parents, prepares to fight another disabled opponent. But Florida authorities deemed the contest a health risk. Can G Money win the right to fight and challenge perceptions of what adaptive athletes can do?

programme.title: Home match
programme.desc: One crucial year in the life of Alina Shilova, a young Ukrainian woman born and raised in the poor suburbs of Kiev, who is torn between playing football and looking after her family.

programme.title: Gay, black and HIV positive: America's hidden epidemic
programme.desc: If you are a black, gay man in America, your risk of contracting HIV is one in two. Leah Green travels to Atlanta, Georgia, which has the largest gay and black community in the country. She finds out how stigma, education and structural racism continue to feed into this startling statistic

programme.title: Happy birthday
programme.desc: For years, global music publisher Warner/Chappell claimed copyright of the Happy Birthday song, demanding payment for any public performance of it. Jenn Nelson tells the story of her four-year campaign to prove that the company did not in fact own the rights to the world-famous song, whose tune was composed by two sisters in Kentucky in 1893. A judge's decision on 30 June to approve a $14m settlement means the song is now in the public domain

programme.title: Why is big tobacco booming? | Campaign to reclaim children's play space on British streets
programme.desc: Smoking rates are falling in the UK, US and much of Europe. Forty-five per cent of Brits smoked in the 60s and 70s, compared with just 15% today. You would think this was bad news for cigarette profits, but tobacco companies are making more money than ever. They claim they no longer market traditional cigarettes, but behind-the scenes tactics suggest otherwise. | There has been a sharp decline in children's outdoor play, leading some experts to point to the growing mental and physical health crisis affecting young people as a possible consequence. Now families are campaigning for children to have fun outdoors again.

programme.title: The Winterkeeper: a lifetime spent protecting the wilderness
programme.desc: Steven Fuller is a winter caretaker who has lived at Yellowstone national park for the past 50 years. As the cold weather approaches and the seasonal transformation begins, he hunkers down in his remote mountain cabin. But Fuller and Yellowstone face an uncertain future, with the climate crisis threatening to change one of North America's last great wildernesses for ever.

programme.title: How to make a pearl
programme.desc: For 53 years, John Kapellas enjoyed the bright sky of the western US, but one day he started to burn, blister and break out in rashes whenever he was exposed to light. Now allergic to the entire spectrum of light, Kapellas has spent the last decade living in complete darkness. When one of the side effects of the drugs John was prescribed made him ‘go nuts', he started drawing large abstract pictures on his walls and playing the piano to calm his mind and soothe the pain.

programme.title: The Tower next door
programme.desc: In June 2017 residents of the Silchester estate woke to discover Grenfell Tower engulfed in flames, witnessing a terrifying national tragedy unfolding on their doorstep. The tower bore a striking resemblance to their own. The victims were people they knew, people from all walks of life who, like them, made a home for themselves high up above the clamour of the city. Faced with an uncertain future, the residents of the tower next door are fighting to rebuild their community.

programme.title: The secret world of Asian drag queens | Oklahoma rodeo: 'I can be gay and I can be a cowboy'
programme.desc: The secret world of Asian drag queens | Oklahoma rodeo: 'I can be gay and I can be a cowboy'

programme.title: Love Jihad: India's lethal religious conspiracy theory
programme.desc: The mutilated body of a 24-year-old Muslim, Arbaaz Aftab Mullah, was discovered on a railway track near his home. His family believe he was murdered because of his interfaith relationship with a Hindu woman and that he is one of the latest victims of the 'love jihad' conspiracy theory, which has swept across groups of Hindu nationalists in India. The theory claims that Muslim men are seducing Hindu women and luring them into marriage in order to convert them to Islam.

programme.title: Fish story
programme.desc: Sometime in the 1980s, Caspar Salmon's grandmother was invited to a gathering on the Welsh island of Anglesey, attended exclusively by people with fish surnames. Or so he says. Thirty years later, film-maker Charlie Lyne attempts to sort myth from reality.

programme.title: Project Runway

programme.title: Project Runway

programme.title: Project Runway

programme.title: Project Runway

programme.title: Mad About You S1E1
programme.desc: Paul and Jamie Buchman are young urban newlyweds who try to sustain their marital bliss while sidestepping the hurdles of love in the '90s. When their planned evening alone turns into an impromptu dinner party, Paul and Jamie prove to be very resourceful in finding quality time together.

programme.title: Mad About You S1E2
programme.desc: Paul nervously anticipates an impromptu reunion with an old college flame and nobly invites a curious Jamie to accompany him and Selby. But he can't help competing with Selby for his ex-girlfriend's attention -- while Jamie discovers a secret about the former relationship that even Paul doesn't know.

programme.title: Mad About You S1E3
programme.desc: An apartment-bound Paul and Jamie want to make the most of their Sunday in Manhattan and finally agree to a strict precise agenda. But their best-laid plans are waylaid by wailing relatives, well-intentioned friends and street-clogging parades -- and just getting to 14th street will be a moral victory.

programme.title: Mad About You S1E4
programme.desc: A pouting Paul reluctantly agrees to give up his Saturday to shop for a sofa with Jamie. But their sofa safari hits two snags when Paul objects to Jamie's choice of a "love seat" and when he learns that his good buddy Dr. Mark Devanow was Jamie's gynecologist.

programme.title: Mad About You S1E5
programme.sub-title: I'M Just So Happy For You
programme.desc: Paul and Jamie both anxiously await the results of professional projects that could further their respective careers: For him, it's a TV documentary he directed, for her, it's a ludicrous public relations proposal. But when Jamie lands her account and a TV network rejects Paul's film, he struggles to put on a happy face during her raucous celebratory party.

programme.title: Mad About You S1E6
programme.desc: Paul feels like the odd man out when Jamie's exceedingly picky parents come to visit. And while her handy dad shows mechanically challenged Paul how to remove a hair clog from the plumbing, Jamie shudders every time her parents question her choices.

programme.title: Mad About You S1E7
programme.desc: Jamie persuades Paul to face an old film school rival, Howie, who blames Paul for derailing his film career and forcing him to become a subway token-taker. After an unpleasant reunion at the subway station, Howie stomps outs, leaving a guilt-ridden Paul and Jamie to cover for him on the job.

programme.title: Mad About You S1E8
programme.desc: Paul and Jamie hire a Russian maid to clean their cluttered apartment, but Paul's nagging guilt compels him to get in the way -- and he finds himself in trouble when she misinterprets his kindness as flirtation.

programme.title: Mad About You S1E9
programme.desc: A reluctant Paul finally accepts Jamie's dare to give up the rent-controlled lease to his old bachelor apartment and sever all connections to the past. But Jamie doesn't know about Paul's edgy tenant -- a strange gawky man named Kramer.

programme.title: Mad About You S1E10
programme.desc: Paul and Jamie's first encounter with their new neighbors is a disaster.

programme.title: Mad About You S1E11
programme.desc: Paul and Jamie get on the wrong track en route to a holiday disaster when they cram aboard a train to visit her parents for their first Thanksgiving together. Their good cheer is derailed when their crowded car is also boarded by their feuding neighbors, the Devanows, neurotic Lisa and freeloading Selby.

programme.title: Mad About You S1E12
programme.desc: When Jamie lands a huge account to promote New York tourism, she eagerly hires Paul to direct the filming of a TV commercial. But when the tourism director disagrees with Paul's artistic vision for the spot, Jamie is caught in the middle and must choose between a stubborn client and her equally immovable husband.

programme.title: Mad About You S1E13
programme.desc: In a flashback from three years earlier, bachelor Paul accidentally bumps into attractive stranger Jamie and uses her misplaced dry cleaning ticket to track her down at her office Christmas party to ask her out for their first date.

programme.desc: A team of six contestants, led by an eccentric Maze Master, journey through four fantastical time zones.

programme.title: Scapa Flow: Graveyard Of The German Fleet
programme.desc: A journey back to 1919 and the remains of one of the world's most spectacular underwater disasters.

programme.title: Hawaii Volcanoes National Park
programme.desc: Hawaii Volcanoes National Park: The Hawaii Volcanoes National Park is the site of strange events. Are they linked to the legendary Native Hawaiian Goddess of the Volcano, Pele?

programme.title: Joshua Tree National Park
programme.desc: Joshua Tree National Park: Are UFO sightings at Joshua Tree linked to an anti-aging machine and a powerful creature called the Yucca Man?

programme.title: Glacier National Park
programme.desc: Glacier National Park: In Montana's Glacier National Park, some claim that there is a hidden underground Nazi base that may even have been the final hideout for Hitler himself.

programme.title: Mount Shasta National Forest
programme.desc: Mount Shasta National Forest: From inexplicable disappearances to mysterious experiments, events in Mount Shasta National Park may be explained by an ancient Karuk Indian legend.

programme.title: Fake, The Spy And The Steam Powered Brain, The
programme.desc: A look at the 'perfect listening bug' and a steam-powered 180-year-old mechanical engine that can solve complex math problems.

programme.title: Ice Age Shaman, The Bronze Age Sky And The Steampunk Lightbulb, The
programme.desc: A look at the 11,000 year old skill of a deer, a decorated Bronze Age disk and a steampunk light bulb.

programme.title: The Observers
programme.desc: The Observers is a mind altering timely comprehensive conversation that plumbs the depths of the UFO phenomenon and asks the hard questions at the heart of this global enigma.

programme.title: Dragons' Den CA
programme.desc: In business, it's all about the pitch – that elusive ability of an entrepreneur to stand tall, talk smart, and capture an audience whenever and wherever opportunity knocks. Each week, with big investment dollars on the line, real-life business owners must pitch like a pro under the dead-eyed scrutiny of five self-made business titans, known as 'The Dragons'. Succeed in this high stakes environment and the reward could be life changing, as the aspiring entrepreneur not only secures financing but the prestige of a coveted Dragon as a mentor and partner. Sold in every continent, Dragons' Den shows viewers around the world the inspiring stories of hard-working business owners and the transformational power of entrepreneurship.

programme.title: The Travel Bug
programme.desc: In this episode Morgan heads to Cairns, northern Queensland. From The Great Barrier Reef and rainforest of the Daintree to Port Douglas and Cape Tribulation, this episode is one for the adventurous travellers!

programme.title: The Travel Bug
programme.desc: In this episode Morgan is kicking off an adventure through Japan. A train ride from Tokyo takes him to the mountainous interior and the ski resorts of Zao and Appi before heading south to the tsunami devastated region of Sendai.

programme.title: The Travel Bug
programme.desc: Still on his Japanese adventure, Morgan hops on a flight south to the warm and sunny island paradise of Okinawa.Despite only being a few hours flight from Tokyo, it seem like they're a world away –It's a hidden gem you're gonna fall in love with!

programme.title: Dropped
programme.desc: Tensions run high as Chris and Casey disagree on what their next course of action should be. Without clear cache directions, the brothers are left with a hard choice. Should they stay, or should they go?

programme.title: Dropped
programme.desc: Chris and Casey hike the mountainside, but the steep slopes and shifting shale make the trek anything but easy. The brothers face energy depletion and exhaustion, and they feel the pressure to find new food sources soon, or go hungry.

programme.title: Dropped
programme.desc: The raft continues to cause problems as Chris and Casey spend valuable energy trying to stay afloat. With their days in the South Pacific dwindling, the brothers have reached their breaking point…until a new discovery leaves a lasting impact.

programme.title: Dropped
programme.desc: Chris and Casey have hiked across New Zealand for weeks. Fighting for their lives has taken a toll on their emotions and abilities. They team up to hunt feral pigs, but the cache items used for survival create new challenges and deep frustrations.

programme.title: Dropped
programme.desc: Chris and Casey Keefer must push to reach the LZ with a failing raft. With only a few hours before extraction, will Mother Nature get the best of them before they can get to safety?

programme.title: Survival Science
programme.desc: Laura and Nick travel to a state-of-the-art Direct Effects Lightning Test Lab. With some “explosive” experiments, they'll determine the power lightning and how it behaves? And they'll find out how can hunters and fishermen best protect themselves?

programme.title: Survival Science
programme.desc: Nick and Laura experience Nature's fury through storm amazing generating gear that simulates terrible hurricanes, ice cold winds and even tornados. For these experiments they'll use remarkable technology that creates massive storms on Hollywood sets.

programme.title: Survival Science
programme.desc: Laura and Nick explore the techniques to deal with these apex predators when they are the most dangerous. We'll scientifically breakdown the behaviors, strength, eyesight and speed of mountain lions through outrageous experiments, in day and night.

programme.title: Survival Science
programme.desc: Nick and Laura head deep into the mountains to explore survival techniques that go far beyond anything they've learned before. Our courageous hosts subject themselves to intense survival training with the Survival Training School of California.

programme.title: Fight to Survive
programme.desc: While solo climbing Mt. Goddard, a remote peak in the California Sierra, Gregg Hein's leg is crushed by a boulder and he's sent careening down the mountain. Barely able to stop his fall, he's now faced with a horrific compound fracture.

programme.title: Fight to Survive
programme.desc: After his leg is crushed by a boulder, Gregg Hein is stranded on a high Sierra peak with an infected compound fracture. Knowing he won't be reported missing for days, Gregg attempts to crawl off the mountain and rescue himself.

programme.title: Fight to Survive
programme.desc: On deployment in Anbar Province, Marine Corp Master Sergeant Buck Doyle throws himself in harm's way to save a fallen comrade from an Iraqi sniper and becomes a target himself. This is a story of survival on and off the battlefield.

programme.title: World Spas Chiva Som, Thailand
programme.desc: From an underwater therapy to a massage that detoxes internal organs, Jamie Srubis tries varied spa treatments at the Chiva Som resort in Thailand.

programme.title: XP Guide Oslo 1, Norway
programme.desc: Oslo is the oldest of the Scandinavian capitals with a rich history dating back to over 1000 years, the city's iconic monuments reveal its evolution through the ages to visitors. Elisha goes on a tour to see some of them such as the magnificent Arkeshus Castle, a medieval castle that was built to protect Oslo but was used instead as a prison, and Oslo City Hall, the administrative center of the city which hosts the annual Nobel peace prize ceremony.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun Toronto, Canada
programme.desc: Alex Drobin gets to know the vibe of Toronto at significant spots such as Saint Lauren's Market and Sir Henry's Garage for Classic Cars.

programme.title: Unwind Banyan tree, Maldives
programme.desc: Indeya unwinds at the Banyan Tree resort. She tries water sports, learns a traditional Maldivian recipe, rejuvenates herself at a spa and ends her day by exploring the capital city of Male.

programme.title: Best From The Rest Top 5 Experiences Ireland
programme.desc: Unlock the wow-factor of Ireland with our top picks for some of the ultimate unforgettable experiences to be had in the country.

programme.title: Landmarks Ha Long Bay, Vietnam
programme.desc: Made up of about 1600 islands and islets huddled together in a lush corner of the Tonkin Bay, off Vietnam's north-east coast, Ha Long Bay is not only a UNESCO World Heritage site, but also central to the mythical and political idea of Vietnam.

programme.title: XP Guide_Oslo 2, Norway
programme.desc: Perhaps the most famous period in Norwegian history was the Viking Age. Far from just barbaric, axe-wielding invaders, the Vikings were Norse seafarers who left a major impact on European history through trade, colonization, and far-flung exploration. During Elisha's visit to Oslo, she finds herself in awe of the extraordinary well-designed Viking ships at a museum, where she comes to know fascinating stories about the lives led by the Vikings.

programme.title: 10 Days South Africa Cape Town
programme.desc: Alex Outhwaite travels to South Africa to know more about its history, and while there, experiences a hoard of things that she had never done before.

programme.title: City Breaks Bundi
programme.desc: Bundi casts a spell on Asmita, when she goes there for a minibreak. She discovers that this forgotten 14th century town in Rajasthan is a treasure waiting to be explored. Right from its mural-filled palaces and tranquil lakes to its narrow lanes with blue houses and its ornate stepwells, she finds herself basking in the romance of the town that takes her back in time.

programme.title: Backpack Tel Aviv, Israel
programme.desc: Sabrina visits Israel and first explores Sarona, its local market, tries local food and wine. She later learns about Jewish settlements in Tel Aviv. She also visits Jaffa and explores its old buildings and flea market and ends with Shakshuka Shushka, a traditional dish.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun Banff, Canada
programme.desc: Alex Drobin escapes to the rugged beauty of Banff, Canada's first national park, where he explores the Cave and Basin National Historic Site, goes to Bow Valley Parkway to spot wildlife and has a cowboy experience at Banff Trail Riders. He also tries smoked buffalo flatbread and beer from a local brewery.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun Dubai 1
programme.desc: Alex explores Dubai from Burj Khalifa, tries a traditional Emirati platter, visits the gold market and enjoys at teg Mall of Emirates and ends with a camel safari and camping that night.

programme.title: 10 Days South Africa Durban
programme.desc: Alex Outhwaite takes a helicopter ride over Drakensberg mountain, and explores the untouched wilderness of Kagga Kamma Nature Reserve.

programme.title: Backpack Caesarea, Israel
programme.desc: Sabrina visits Zodiac fountain, Jaffa Port followed by exploring the National Park in Caesarea, Roman theatre built by King Herod and concludes her trip after her visit to the shrine of Bab, learning about the Baha'i faith.

programme.desc: Hope's busy season arrives early. Two patients from last year are released.

programme.desc: Follow Dr. Scott as he saves a hedgehog's life and gives a parrot a manicure.

programme.desc: Help a pup with a long list of troubles and a terrier in deep trouble.

programme.desc: Prep for a pup's leg surgery, inspect kitty ears, and check on a Seeing Eye dog.

programme.title: A Modern Romance: Harry & Meghan
programme.desc: The remarkable lives of actress Meghan Markle and Prince Harry.

programme.title: William and Kate: A Royal Love Story
programme.desc: For the ten year anniversary, this documentary is the definitive account of the love story between William and the girl who is to become his Queen.

programme.title: Japan's Royal Hereditary System
programme.desc: The Japanese Imperial family is the oldest hereditary monarchy in the world, dating back to the sixth century BC. However, its central role in Japan has not shielded the family from controversy in recent years. Through personal scandals, and vagaries of the hereditary system, even to their own biology, it's been a rocky time. But what is left if every tradition is changeable?

programme.title: Inside the Royal Firm
programme.desc: From footmen and butlers to bodyguards, nannies and private secretaries this film tells the stories of the British royal family's long-suffering servants.

programme.title: UN Under-Secretary-General
programme.desc: This is Westminster, the House of Parliament in London. Its oldest and most exclusive institution, is the House of Lords. Here, for centuries and generations, members of the British landed aristocracy have been holding on to their pomp, duties and privileges. Today the Leader of the House of Lords is a woman. On top of it, she's black. An extraordinary story and quite a character. Come and meet Baroness Amos of Brondesbury.

programme.title: Queen Margrethe Of Denmark: Her Private Life
programme.desc: The private life of the Queen of Denmark. In 2020, Queen Margrethe will celebrate her 80th birthday - an excellent opportunity to look back at Her Majesty's more unofficial side and explore her vast knowledge of art, culture and history.

programme.title: British Royals & National Socialism
programme.desc: The film presents documents and footage proving the royal family's and leading British aristocrats' sympathy for National Socialism.

programme.title: FashionTV Global
programme.desc: FashionTV L'Original

programme.title: FashionTV Global
programme.desc: FashionTV L'Original

programme.title: The Grateful Dud
programme.desc: At the Country Club where she teaches horseback riding after school, Mia's heroic rescue of a puppy and horse earns the overwhelming gratitude of their owner, a teen bulldozer named Fiona. But when Fiona's pushy, over-the-top appreciation makes Mia's life a living nightmare, the Friends must Ride To The Rescue! At the Country Club where she teaches horseback riding after school, Mia's heroic rescue of a puppy and horse earns the overwhelming gratitude of their owner, a brassy teen bulldozer named Fiona. Although Fiona means well, her pushy, over-the-top appreciation soon makes Mia's life a living nightmare: embarrassing Mia at school, scaring away her boyfriend, and causing major havoc at the Country Club. The Friends must find a way for Mia to escape Fiona's nonstop gratitude and foil Tanya's sneaky schemes to get Mia kicked out of the club. And when a crisis erupts at the club's Fall Ball and Horse Show, all the Friends have to do some fancy riding to help their BFF save the day.

programme.title: Black Out
programme.desc: The power is out in all of Ninjag, but the hard drive containing the Digital Overlord has disappeared. Some mysterious Stranger has stolen it and is now sending alternatively powered Nindroids to attack the Ninja and capture Lloyd. After destroying the Power Station, the Ninja have dealt a massive blow to the Nindroids, but find getting back to the city troublesome without power. At the Junk Yard, Zane gives half of his heart to revive Pixal to help give guidance in their greater mission. Meanwhile, a mysterious cloaked Interloper steals the hard drive containing the Digital Overlord and creates a new foe for the Ninja to fight… Tech Wu. On the other side of Ninjago, Lloyd is trained by his father to harness his Golden Power to unlock an even greater potential – the power of the First Spinjtzu Master.

programme.title: The Curse Of The Golden Master
programme.desc: The Stranger and the hard drive with the Digital Overlord are still missing. The ninjas' search for them brings them deep underground where they are assisted by an old enemy. But the Stranger and his Nindroids are getting ever closer to capturing Lloyd. The Ninja return to a powerless New Ninjago City and visit the Serpentine Tombs in effort to solve who had taken the hard drive containing the Digital Overlord – only to discover the snakes have gone straight. They learn of Serpentine Lore that a Golden Master will conquer all of Ninjago. The Nindroids attack, but with the help from Scales and his Serpentine ilk, they fight off the enemy and discover the culprit they are after is none other than Pythor! But it's too late… the Digital Overlord and his henchman have captured Lloyd in a plot to sap his Golden Power to finally become flesh.

programme.title: Enter The Digiverse
programme.desc: Pythor the Anachondrai and his Nindroids have captured Lloyd and are draining his golden powers to bring the Overlord back into Ninjago. And the Ninja must use the Techno Blades in a daring rescue … inside the Overlord's corrupted hard drive. Unable to save Lloyd in time, the Ninja resort to drastic measures. With the help from Borg's advanced technology, the Ninja get digitized with their Techno Blades into the Digiverse in order to battle the Digital Overlord on his own turf and erase evil for good. While the Ninja learn new capabilities and explore the fantasy of a ‘digital world', their friends must protect them from Pythor and his army in the ‘real world'. In the climatic conclusion, the Ninja ‘reboot the system' and save Sensei Wu and Lloyd, but the Digital Overlord has managed to become flesh in the nick of time, only to be in a weakened state.

programme.title: The Invitation
programme.desc: When the Ninja receive an invitation to partake in the Tournament of Elements and a clue that Zane might be alive, they accept and travel to a Lost Island owned by notorious crime lord Master Chen in order to save their friend. The loss of Zane has fractured the team of Ninja, but when a mysterious invitation to Master Chen's ‘Tournament of Elements' arrives, everything is thrown into question – Is Zane alive, and are there others out there with powers like them? Sensei Garmadon warns them not to trust Master Chen, but they go in order to find Zane and make the team whole. On route to the island, the Ninja are briefly introduced to the other unique fighters with elemental powers, and the mysterious and beautiful Skylor intrigues Kai. Kai gets into a humbling scrap with Karlof, the Master of Metal, and learns some fights must be done on your own.

programme.title: The Book Of Obsession
programme.desc: The Knights are tasked with protecting the King's valuable Ned Knights comic book during the Bookingheim Knight-a-Con. But with all the costumed kids there, will they recognize the real Jestro and Book of Monsters coming to get them? King Halbert instructs the Knights that they must take his valuable Ned Knights comic book to the “Knight-a-Kon” in Bookingham. It will be the first be first prize at the Knight-a-Kon. But the Book of Monsters wants the book, too. Having devoured Ned Knightly comic books in it's down time it has become fanatically obsessed with the storyline. General Magmar suggests they simply attack the place and take what they want, but Jestro tells them they can't: everyone will be in costume and they'll be thousands of comics there. This calls for a clever “infiltration” mission (which will also allow Jestro to see the Kon, which he's always wanted to do.) So Jestro goes into the Kon with the BOM and some monsters. And it's easy because everyone is in costume Jestro, the BOM and their monsters fit right in at the convention and Clay and crew have their work cut out for them trying to separate fiend from fan. Macy keeps going on-and-on about the story in the King's comic book as the Knights keep a watchful eye on it. Robin even shows up in the “Minitrex” – a small version of the Fortrex he built just for the Knight-a-Kon. He's convinced he can win the costume competition that is the big closing event for the Kon. At the end of the Kon there's a big awards ceremony for “Best Costume.” The prize: “Ned Knightly, Knight of Knighton, Issue #1.” The BOM must get it and scheme to get closer to it during the costume contest. He's just…about…to get the book when…the impatient Magmar attacks! Everyone thinks it's part of the big show! “Ned Knightly, Knight of Knighton, Issue #1” is tossed from person-to-person-to-person in the melee. The Knights must do something, but they're too far away from the Fortrex to download any NEXO-Power. Robin pulls up in his Minitrex they can use it to get NEXO-Power! Robin wins the best costume for his Minitrex and wins the Ned Knightly Comic which he then gives to Macy…she deserves to read it.

programme.title: The King's Tournament
programme.desc: Rival to the King, Jorah Tightwad thinks his knights – the Tighty Knighties – are easily better than the NEXO-Knights, so he challenges King Halbert to prove who's better during the King's Birthday Tournament. It's the King's birthday! To celebrate, the Queen and Macy always throw him a great, big birthday party consisting of a feast on birthday eve and a tournament the next day. At the birthday banquet is JORAH TIGHTWAD (last seen in episode 104.) He's goading the King (and on his birthday, even) and going on and on about how his knights are better than the NEXO-Knights. The King protests, but Jorah asks “How many magical books have your knights recovered? None, zilch, zero.” But he produces the BOOK OF ENVY, which his superior knights captured from Jestro. The next day, the NEXO-Knights square off against Tightwad's four knights: JOUSTING BEEBER, BRICKNEY SPEARS, SHIA LABLADE and THE BLOK. We see that all these knights are envious of the NEXO-Knights: their weapons, their fame, their hair, etc. Macy is also disappointed to find out she CAN'T fight in the tournament – she's agreed to be in “princess mode” for her father's birthday. During the tournament, we see that Tightwad's knights are amazing in fighting contests. They all pair-off with knights who have their same weapons skills. Tightwad makes fun of the NEXO-Knights as his knights do well. Jestro and the Book of Monsters discover that Tightwad has the Book of Envy and he and his monsters attack the tournament. Then we learn that Tightwad's knights might be good tournament fighters, but they can't handle real fights and the NEXO-Knights must save them! Macy is finally able to join in the fight when she sees Magmar show up on the flank. The NEXO-Knights successfully defend the king, but Jestro gets away with the Book of Envy. Tightwad must admit his knights never won the book from Jestro he got the book when Merlok blasted books of magic across the realm. Tightwad's knights all admit that they really admire the NEXO-Knights.

programme.title: Monster Chef
programme.desc: Axl and Chef Éclair must enter a cooking contest to try to keep Jestro from getting the hottest chili recipe in the realm. Knighton celebrity chef GOBBLETON RAMBLEY announces that he's developed the hottest chili recipe ever created. Now, he intends to give the recipe to the winner of his “Hottest Chili Cook-Off Competition” cooking show on Knighton TV. He'll turn over his famous Chili Cookbook with the hottest chili recipe on the last page. It's so unique it can't be recreated without it. Jestro and the Book of Monsters think they MUST have that recipe. “Hot chili makes hot monsters!” Merlok knows that this recipe, in the wrong hands – meaning Jestro's – could be dangerous. But when the Knights appeal to the egomaniacal chef, he says the only way to get the recipe is to win it from him. So Axl and Chef Éclair decide to enter. The team that creates the hottest chili will win Rambley's cookbook. Chef Éclair is unsure that Axl has the skills to help them win the competition, but, as we see through funny cooking show bits, Axl is quite good and they become a pretty good team. (We will also learn that years ago, Rambley stole one of Éclair's best recipes and they don't like each other.) While watching the multi-part competition, Aaron and Lance have a contest of their own: who can eat the hottest chili. Axl and Éclair actually win the competition, coming up with a raging hot chili recipe. Before they can claim their prize, Jestro and his monsters attack with Magmar leading the way on his siege tower. They must get that cookbook! During the battle, Rambley's cookbook is accidentally melted. After the battle, Rambley announces his new quest: to make the sweetest ice cream ever! While Axl and Éclair cook happily in the kitchen of the Fortrex, back in the Redoubt of Ruination, Jestro and Lavaria are attempting to recreate Rambley's chili recipe. They keep spooning various recipes of chili into the Book of Monsters. “Nope, not hot enough…waaaay too much garlic….you call that hot?!” (BELCH!)

programme.title: Knight Out
programme.desc: When the other NEXO-Knights decide they need a vacation from Clay, they send him on a holiday not knowing that he's going to a resort that houses an evil book or that Jestro will be heading there, too. Clay needs a vacation. Or, actually, the knights need a vacation from Clay! Clay is so super-focused that sometimes they just can't take it: training, training, maintenance, the Knights' Code tests then…more training. Even Merlok 2.0 is exhausted and he has no physical form! So, the Knights send Clay off on a holiday against his will (arranged by Lance because if there's one thing he can do it's plan a vacation.) But unbeknownst to the other Knights, Jestro and the Book of Monsters are tracking an evil book. A book that they suspect is located near the resort “Exalted Towers” renowned for its exquisite service, where Clay is staying. It's the BOOK OF CRUELTY and Clay notices that it's affecting the “high class” resort where Lance has booked him: the service is terrible, the staff is nasty, and everyone seems to take joy in being cruel to him (and to each other.) Meanwhile, the other Knights are having a terrible time because they realize that Clay takes charge of the basic maintenance of almost everything in the Fortrex. Stuff starts breaking down, chores are not getting done and everyone is getting on each other's nerves. Clay senses things are “off” at the resort, and discovers the book they've been using as the guest register is really the Book of Cruelty. As Clay scoops it up, he looks outside to see…Jestro and his monsters arrive on the Evilmobile. Clay battles as best he can on his own by setting traps and being cunning but he's finally caught. Just when it looks like this will be the cruelest vacation ever the other Knights arrive on their vehicles! They spring into action, free Clay, and battle and defeat Jestro and the monsters – though Jestro escapes. The Knights make Clay promise to never go on vacation again.

programme.title: Cheat Code
programme.desc: Competing over who can ask Zoey to join their Sheep Week team, Logan cheats and unwittingly endangers the video game based Cyber Realm.

programme.title: The Bigger Picture
programme.desc: When the kids meddle in their teacher's dream to find test answers, they realize that in dreams, sweet little Ms. Putnam isn't so sweet... or little!

programme.title: Beasts And Dragons, Rise Of Garmadon
programme.desc: Zane, Jay, and Kai remember some of Ninjago's fearsome creatures so that Cole defend the cave from an unidentified monster. But Ninja confront memories of their greatest villain and ally, Garmadon, before the threat to Zane controls him completely.

programme.title: The Oni And The Dragon
programme.desc: While Princess Harumi learns to adjust to the life of a Ninja, Lloyd and Jay search for answers about the Oni Masks at Mystake's Tea house, while Cole and Zane attempt to infiltrate the Sons of Garmadon biker gang. Remembering that Lloyd managed to arrest one of the gang members at Borg Tower, the Ninja decide to interrogate him to ascertain the location of the third Oni Mask. In order to get him to talk, however, Cole suggests they visit MYSTAKE'S TEA HOUSE to obtain some "truth tea." The truth-tea backfires spectacularly when Cole accidentally switches cups and drinks the tea himself. Zane is able to salvage the situation, however, and obtains a lead. He learns that the Sons of Garmadon frequent a karaoke bar called LAUGHY'S. Zane and Cole don disguises to infiltrate the bar. Cole assumes the alter ego “Rocky Dangerbuff," while Zane adopts the subtler moniker "Snake Jaguar." In order to earn the gang's trust, Cole performs a karaoke routine that is a smash success. Unfortunately, during the routine, his disguise comes loose and he is exposed as a ninja. The Sons of Garmadon attack him, and Zane (who is still in disguise) is unable to intervene. Cole is taken prisoner while Zane is invited back to the Sons of Garmadon's secret base.

programme.title: The Pranks
programme.desc: The friends are using their talents to pull a prank on Olivia. But who gets the last laugh?Stephanie finds the perfect front page story for the school paper! It's almost too good to be true. Emma and Andrea go undercover to solve the mystery of the two Jacobs.

programme.title: Getting Out the Vote
programme.desc: It's Stephanie vs. Tanya for Heartlake High Class President. But when Tanya resorts to dirty tricks, Stephanie decides to give her a taste of her own medicine. Elect Tanya as Heartlake High Class President?! "NOOOOOOOO!!!!" Not if Friends Stephanie, Emma, Olivia, Mia and Andrea can help it. The Friends are there to help their pal Matthew defeat Tanya -- even when Emma gets carried off by "Vote For Matthew" balloons! But Tanya figures since "Daddy" is now the Mayor of Heartlake City, she doesn't have to play by the rules. When she sneakily sabotages Matthew's campaign, it's time for a determined Stephanie to take his place as candidate. With the help of her friends, Stephanie is set on running a clean campaign -- that is, until Tanya resorts to more dirty tricks, and Stephanie decides to give Tanya a taste of her own medicine.

programme.title: Getting Tthe Message
programme.desc: As the Friends enjoy Heartlake Amusement Park, Emma types a text to Mia, but sends it to Matthew by mistake a text that could be mega-embarrassing for Emma! As her BFF's, the Friends race to find Matthew's phone and erase the text before he reads it. But first, they must brave space rides, dunking booths and worse: the boys locker room! What's more frightening than a roller coaster? Moretwisted than a funhouse mirror? A text gone wrong! TheFriends' fun day at Heartlake Amusement Park turns into afrantic race against time when Emma discovers that herinnocent text to Mia was sent to Matthew by mistake -- atext that accidentally sounds like a pledge of Emma'sundying love for Matthew! Mega-embarrassing! As Emma'sBFF's, the Friends must scramble to retrieve Matthew'sphone and erase the text before he reads it. But first,they need to brave space rides, dunking booths, an angrybrute and worse - the boys locker room!

programme.title: The Grateful Dud
programme.desc: At the Country Club where she teaches horseback riding after school, Mia's heroic rescue of a puppy and horse earns the overwhelming gratitude of their owner, a teen bulldozer named Fiona. But when Fiona's pushy, over-the-top appreciation makes Mia's life a living nightmare, the Friends must Ride To The Rescue! At the Country Club where she teaches horseback riding after school, Mia's heroic rescue of a puppy and horse earns the overwhelming gratitude of their owner, a brassy teen bulldozer named Fiona. Although Fiona means well, her pushy, over-the-top appreciation soon makes Mia's life a living nightmare: embarrassing Mia at school, scaring away her boyfriend, and causing major havoc at the Country Club. The Friends must find a way for Mia to escape Fiona's nonstop gratitude and foil Tanya's sneaky schemes to get Mia kicked out of the club. And when a crisis erupts at the club's Fall Ball and Horse Show, all the Friends have to do some fancy riding to help their BFF save the day.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Bumblebee competes against the Decepticons in an underground racing competition.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: When the AllSpark turns construction vehicles into Autobots, Bulkhead becomes fast friends.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Prowl goes after Starscream when he escapes from the custody of the Elite Guard.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: When the team visit the local museum, they all think Whirl's gone into police overdrive.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Hot Shot beats his bad luck with the help of Cody's good luck charm - a rubber ducky.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.sub-title: Did You See What I Thaw?
programme.desc: The Bots discover a caveman preserved in ice.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Kade's favorite movie monster materializes in Griffin Rock.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Unlikely duos team up, including Acree and Wheeljack, and Agent Fowler and Jack's mom.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Knock Out and Starscream experiment with synthetic energon.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Megatron doubts Predaking and his allegiance after Predaking reveals a secret of his own.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Not wanting the over-aggressive Dinobot to compromise his team's quest.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: The Scavengers find a most unexpected relic.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Starscream gloats over his victory, unaware that Optimus Prime has arrived to help the remaining Autobots.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Robots are truly in disguise when the Autobots pose as Stunticons to uncover Megatron's latest plan.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: The Autobots participate in a charity road race to watch over an experimental race car.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: The Aerialbots are thrown back in time to Cybertron's Golden Age.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Papa's Flying Bed: Gargamel casts a spell on Papa's new bed so anyone who sleeps in it is transported to the location of his dream. The drowsy Smurfs' fantasies lead them to a private ship, a castle and finally to Gargamel's. They escape when Clumsy falls asleep and dreams of home.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Lazy's Nightmare: Every time Lazy falls asleep, he is caught in the same recurrent nightmare: he is in the power of Morphio, an evil sorcerer who wants to capture as many sleeping souls as possible. One after another, all the Smurfs are going to fall in Morphio's clutches. All of them? Well, maybe not, if Papa Smurf can help it. And Papa Smurf goes to the Sandman for help...

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Smurfic Games: The Smurfs discover the spirit and joy of friendly competition when they hold their first "Smurfic Games". After a near miss with the troublesome cat Azrael, the Smurfs decide they need to get into better shape. Papa Smurf suggests that the Smurfs tone their bodies as well as sharpen their athletic abilities by inaugurating the "Smurfic Games".

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - A Mere Truffle: The Smurfs love truffles but truffles belong to the Trolls and the Trolls live under Truffle Troll Mountain, where in the shadows of the lake lurks the Thing with No Name...

programme.title: Smurfing For Ghosts
programme.desc: Peewit is at the castle of his friends Selwyn and Tallulah when Uncle Fenwick, the friendly ghost, asks for his help in chasing a few relatives who've come to haunt him... Peewit has to get help from the Smurfs, and a merry chase begins...

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Journey To The Centre Of The Smurf: Winter is setting in on the Village of the Smurfs. The little Smurfs are freezing. But what is Clumsy doing with this strange rock he's discovered? And why is Miner Smurf so enthusiastic about their new discovery? Surely, one more addition to Clumsy's rock collection is not worth all this hoopla... The Smurfs will discover danger and surprises when they go underground.

programme.title: A Bell For Azrael
programme.desc: Gargamel puts a spell on a bell attached to Azrael's tail that will lead the cat to the Smurfs. Papa Smurf outwits him with a counter-spell that leads the wizard to Big Mouth instead of the tasty Smurfs.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - The Gingerbread Smurfs: Brainy feels up to the occasion when he starts the recipe for cookies. He is sure to win the contest: he borrowed a recipe from Papa Smurf's Book of Magic. But a big surprise, and some nasty happenings, await poor Brainy... and the whole Village.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Papa's Wedding Day: When the flower fairy Nymph arrives at the Smurf Village, all the Smurfs fall for her charm and wit. And soon they learn that she is to be some sort of Mama Smurf to them...

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - The Incredible Shrinking Wizard: Gargamel has thrown in his slime pond so much poisonous muck that the water becomes enchanted and Gargamel, after falling in, suddenly finds himself much smaller that he would wish. Actually he is going to be so small that Papa Smurf will save him!

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Greedy Goes On Strike: Get up early in the morning, before any other Smurf is out of bed, to prepare a scrumptious Smurfberry muffin. Spend all morning on preparing Smurf delicacies for lunch. Than all afternoon to have dinner ready – and delicious – on time. And do you get so much as a thank you form the Smurfs? So Greedy Smurf quits. The Village is in trouble... And soon, so is Greedy.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Petrified Smurfs: Brainy Smurf's done it again. This time, in front of Papa Smurf, too. He has recited a spell that changes everything to stone. Everything including himself and all the Smurfs and the Village, and the forest around for miles... Fortunately, Peewit appears....

programme.title: Smurfing In Sign Language
programme.desc: The Smurfs meet a woodland elf, who teaches them sign language after Gargamel inflicts a no-speaking potion on them.

programme.title: The Gargoyle Of Quarrel Castle
programme.desc: In Quarrel Castle, you almost never hear a kind word. Both Selwyn the Warlock and Tallulah, his witch-mate, spend their time quarrelling. Tharp, their pet gargoyle, takes refuge in the Village of Smurfs. But something terrible is going to happen....

programme.title: Jokey's Joke Book
programme.desc: Jokey Smurf's joke boxes are no longer funny to the other Smurfs. Jokey finds new ideas in his Great Joke Book, but these ideas are even less popular in the Village. And here comes the evil Gargamel with a few jokes of his own...

programme.title: Return To Don Smurfo
programme.desc: When a drop of Papa Smurf's new potion spills on the Smurflings' adventure book, the fictional hero springs to life. The dashing Don Smurfo, willing to rid the Smurf of the evil Gargamel, gallantly captures him...

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Flighty's Plight: Flighty Smurf, as his name implies, can't make his own mind up about anything. The other Smurfs take him to the forest for a survival test in which he can find his way home only by making decisions. He ends up in Balthazar's castle.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - No Smurf Is An Island: Handy Smurf is the one who fixes everything that needs mending in the Village of the Smurfs. But are his efforts really appreciated by the other Smurfs? Not at all. At least this is what Handy feels when he resolves to leave the Smurfs Village forever and live in the sea with Marina the Mermaid...

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Born Rotten: As the Smurfs prepare for the Festival of the Stars, Gargamel plants a creature in their midst that smells so rotten that he can easily be traced to the Smurf village. Papa Smurf realizes that Born Rotten is from another planet, and he instructs Feathers to return him to his home before Gargamel finds them.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Never Smurf Off Till Tomorrow: A fierce wind carries the Smurfs' windmill away, with several Smurfs inside. Is Lazy Smurf really at fault or is an injustice being committed? Are the captive Smurfs going to be saved from the volcano's crater? Is Brainy going to smurf up his mouth for a moment?

programme.title: Flamenco Guitar
programme.desc: The Tubbies laugh when laugh when they bump into each other and fall over. They all watch two girls meet Tito a Flamenco guitar player who plays them a nursery rhyme. A guitar appears in Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Fungie the Dolphin
programme.desc: The Teletubbies love jump, but they stop to see Andy Brown and some children take a trip on a boat to see Funghi the Dolphin. The Teletubbies are out in Teletubbyland. When they meet each other, they say hello, making sure no-one is missed out.

programme.title: Going for a Walk
programme.desc: All four Teletubbies go under the slide. The Teletubbies watch some children go over a stile, under some trees and through a gate. When Laa-Laa goes for walk, Tinky Winky and Dipsy secretly follow her.

programme.title: Delilah Packing
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a little girl and her sister getting ready to go away for a day. Tinky Winky puts all the Teletubbies favourite things into his bag.

programme.title: Dirty Dog
programme.desc: The toaster makes an extra piece of Tubby Toast – but who is going to eat it? The Teletubbies watch Emily And Jamie take their dog Jessie for a walk before giving her a bath.

programme.title: Dog Walking
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch two children take their dog for a walk. A voice trumpet appears and barks like a dog. Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po laugh as they run all over Teletubbyland barking.

programme.title: Drawing Cacti
programme.desc: Po feels like having some Tubby Custard. The Teletubbies watch some children visiting a conservatory and drawing cacti. Tinky Winky goes for a walk. Five birds appear in Teletubbyland and the Teletubbies dance round the flowers.

programme.title: Dry Stone Wall
programme.desc: The Teletubbies go up the slide to see two children help mend a dry stone wall. A wall appears in Teletubbyland. Laa-Laa, Po and Tinky Winky play a game with the wall. They hear Dipsy coming and hide behind the wall.

programme.title: Eid, New Clothes
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children try on their new clothes for the Eid festival. A mirror appears in Teletubbyland. Laa-Laa and Dipsy love to look at their reflection, but when Tinky Winky wants to have a go, he's too tall.

programme.title: Emily & Jester
programme.desc: A chair appears in Teletubbyland and Laa-Laa has fun standing up and sitting down. The Teletubbies watch a little girl, Emily, riding her pony, Jester.

programme.title: Emily & Trap
programme.desc: Over the hills and far away, Po loves to ride her scooter fast and slow. The Teletubbies watch Emily ride in her trap, drawn by her pony Jester.

programme.title: Emily Washing Pony
programme.desc: Laa-Laa has fun wearing Dipsy's hat. The Teletubbies watch Emily washing her pony, Jester. The Teletubbies enjoy playing a game washing with their Tubby sponges.

programme.title: Feeding the Chickens
programme.desc: The Teletubbies dance in and out of the trees. The Teletubbies watch Matthew, James and Mark feeding their chickens. There's toast all over Teletubbyland when the Tubby Toaster makes too much toast.

programme.title: Care Bears
programme.desc: Harmony makes a new song, which Cheer shares to the whole of Care-a-Lot before it's finished.

programme.title: Care Bears
programme.desc: Lotsa-Heart Elephant accidentally injures Grumpy, and attempts to heal him.

programme.title: Care Bears
programme.desc: Beastly and Share have trouble operating snack bars right next to each other.

programme.title: Care Bears
programme.desc: Share breaks Grumpy's Cloud Express invention and tries to hide that she did it.

programme.title: Care Bears
programme.desc: Cheer and Share are battling a brand new monster- the green-eyed monster called jealousy!

programme.title: Care Bears
programme.desc: Grumpy rebuilds Amigo Bear's ice cream cart so it provides an endless supply of ice cream.

programme.title: Care Bears
programme.desc: The Care Bears give Funshine a trophy to say "thank you" for his helpfulness, but it ends up going to his head

programme.title: Care Bears
programme.desc: Grumpy is storming around Care-a-lot, grumpier than usual, so Cheer decides to "un-grump the "Grumpster".

programme.title: Care Bears
programme.desc: When a Twinklet, a baby star, gets lost in Care-a-lot, Oopsy decides to adopt her.

programme.title: Care Bears
programme.desc: Share thinks that winning a ribbon in a Belly Ball Game will make her friends like her more.

programme.title: Care Bears
programme.desc: Funshine wants to clean up Rainshine Meadows, which is an environmental disaster, and help its inhabitants.

programme.title: Care Bears
programme.desc: Oopsy wants his friends to like him, not just accept him, so he puts on some love perfume to win them over.

programme.title: Care Bears
programme.desc: Oopsy is feeling un-special and gets tricked by Grizzle to help him deliver a "gift" for the Care Bears.

programme.title: Care Bears
programme.desc: Funshine and Tenderheart are in a daring mood, each one trying to one-up the other with a bigger dare.

programme.title: Care Bears
programme.desc: Grizzle is kicked out of his lair by his new robot! Stuck in Care-a-lot, the bears welcome him with open arms.

programme.title: Lego DREAMZzz
programme.desc: When a vengeful witch capable of stealing memories sets out to undo the dream world, our ragtag band of dream chasers are all that stands in her way.

programme.title: Sunny Bunnies
programme.desc: A bunch of round, fluffy Sunny Bunnies are the best of friends. They are always examining their surrounding and doing something adventurous.

programme.title: Paw Patrol: Pup Tales
programme.desc: The PAW Patrol follows a pack of heroic rescue pups. Together they work to protect the Adventure Bay community knowing no job is too big, no pup is too small!

programme.title: CoComelon: JJ's Animal Time
programme.desc: JJ's best animal pals are back and sillier than ever! Sing along with them on their hilarious adventures.

programme.title: Hello CoComelon!
programme.desc: Say hello to JJ, YoYo and Tom Tom, and all of their friends, as they help children learn counting, colours and life lessons with toe-tapping tunes!

programme.title: Learn with Blippi
programme.desc: Blippi teaches children about the world around them in a fun and exciting way - exploring science, space, arts and crafts and learning numbers, colours, shapes, words and more.

programme.title: Little Baby Bum
programme.desc: Little Baby Bum is a fun and educational show for kids featuring classic and new nursery rhymes and loved by babies and their parents all around the world.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake: Berry in the Big City

programme.title: Pinocchio
programme.desc: Live-action adaptation of the beloved story of a wooden puppet who magically comes to life. Pinocchio longs for adventure and is easily led astray, encountering magical beasts and fantastical spectacles. However his dream is to become a real boy, which can only come true if he changes his ways.

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Kate Winslet (Finding Neverland) and Willem Dafoe (Finding Dory) shine in this heart-warming tale of family and friendship. Manou's world is turned upside down when he realises that he is not a seagull like the rest of his beloved family. Shocked, he runs away from home, where he meets birds of his own species and finds out who he really is. However, when both seagulls and swifts face a dangerous threat, Manou must face his foes and become the hero he was always meant to be.

programme.title: The Skeleton's Compass

programme.title: All Dogs go to Heaven
programme.desc: A poignant and classic, masterpeice animation from acclaimed filmmaker Don Bluth and featuring the voice of Burt Reynolds. A stunning family movie about the canine criminal underworld as we follow a furry outcast, Charlie, who comes back to earth from heaven and befriends a young orphan who can speak to animals. As their friendship blossoms Charlie learns that friendship is the most heavely gift of all.
